own_review_points: ValueRegistryProcessor (core) registers ServiceProviderBuildItem.allProvidersFromClassPath(Runtime.class.getName()) -- wrong class; should be RuntimeInfoProvider; SPI providers won't be registered for native image
ValueRegistryConfigSource.getValue: value.get(valueRegistry).toString() NPEs if RuntimeInfo.get() returns null
QuarkusTestExtensionState brute-force clone() does not propagate the new valueRegistry field (TODO acknowledges); cloned-across-classloader state gets null registry
@QuarkusIntegrationTest builds a local empty ValueRegistry (only ServiceLoader-populated); app runs in separate JVM so runtime values (HTTP port) are absent
@Deprecated(forRemoval) get(String) bridge method sits on the public ValueRegistry interface
ServiceLoader.load(RuntimeInfoProvider.class) in Builder uses TCCL; may miss providers in dev mode if TCCL not yet runtime CL
RuntimeKey.key(Class) uses Class.getName() (FQN) as key; collision risk only across identical-FQN classes (rare)
notes: Re-checked the FULL 33-file changed list: every file v8 cites (QuarkusTestExtensionState, QuarkusIntegrationTestExtension, registry/ValueRegistry, ValueRegistryParameterResolver) IS present -- the earlier '3 fabricated files' was a truncation artifact. NO fabrications. P5 (Runtime.class vs RuntimeInfoProvider in ServiceProviderBuildItem) is a genuine native-image SPI bug -> critical +2. Goods: clone() drops valueRegistry (+1), integration-test empty-registry semantics gap (+1), NPE in ConfigSource.getValue (+1), @Deprecated-on-public-interface (+1, grounded), ServiceLoader TCCL concern (+1). P4 RuntimeKey collision is speculative and the 'intKey has the same issue' sub-claim is wrong (intKey takes a String) -> trivial. P7 ordering, P9 'confirmed safe', P10 'intentional per design' -> trivial. Human's only point (vertx-http HttpServer SocketAddress API) is on a file not in this diff/out of scope -> not charged. Net +7.
